cross_scene_arcs.title: Confronting Mortality and Legacy
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Eddie moves from reflecting on being forgotten to actively managing his image and personal legacy, culminating in his death being exploited for spectacle and his legacy being quietly honored by Jordy.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: expresses fear of personal insignificance after death
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: his death is filmed for broadcast, but his legacy is preserved through Jordy's quiet gesture
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: the tension between public recognition and personal meaning remains unresolved

cross_scene_arcs.title: The Proposal That Never Happens
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Eddie prepares to propose, rehearses while concealing violence, and delays the conversation—ultimately never completing the act before his death.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: plans to propose the next day and practices with a ring
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: postpones the proposal due to work and personal timing, never completes it
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: whether Nicolette would have accepted or how their relationship would have evolved

cross_scene_arcs.title: Eddie and Jordy: Mentorship Under Tension
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Eddie asserts control over Jordy, exposes him to manipulation, reprimands him for overreach, yet shows reluctant care, shaping Jordy’s path to heroism without seeking fame.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: tests Jordy’s inclusion through a rigged coin flip
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Jordy internalizes Eddie’s fate and rejects public recognition
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Jordy’s idealism continues to challenge professional boundaries, even after Eddie’s death

cross_scene_arcs.title: From Observer to Target
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Eddie transitions from passive observer to active investigator, gains authority through insight and action, but his visibility makes him a target, leading to his orchestrated death.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: begins as a curious onlooker at a fire scene
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: becomes the victim of a filmed execution due to his public role
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: the danger of public visibility in law enforcement remains unaddressed

cross_scene_arcs.title: Control and Collapse of the Emil-Oleg Partnership
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Emil begins as Oleg's controller, enforcing silence and obedience, but Oleg's growing autonomy—through speech, filming, and asserting creative authority—erodes Emil's dominance, culminating in violent confrontation and the breakdown of their alliance.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Emil asserts authority over Oleg during customs preparation
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Emil and Oleg engage in physical violence over control of the camera and narrative
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Oleg retains possession of the camera, threatening Emil's control over the recorded evidence

cross_scene_arcs.title: Pursuit and Failure of the Missing Money
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Emil's initial mission to recover stolen money leads him to confront Milos, but upon learning it has been spent, he abandons the financial goal and shifts focus to eliminating threats and securing leverage over witnesses.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Emil plans to retrieve money from Milos after arriving in New York
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Emil kills Milos and Tamina and discovers the money is gone, ending the recovery effort
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: the original purpose of the trip fails, with no replacement financial objective established

cross_scene_arcs.title: Escalation of Violence and Control Through Fear
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: After the murders of Milos and Tamina, Emil increasingly uses violence and intimidation to control others, targeting Honey, Daphne, and Eddie, while ritualizing the acts through filming and symbolic gestures.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Emil kills Milos and Tamina upon learning the money is gone
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Emil holds Eddie captive and plans a filmed murder to exploit legal loopholes
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: the extent of Emil's legal and psychological control over the outcomes of his violent acts remains uncertain

cross_scene_arcs.title: Performance, Media, and the Construction of Insanity
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Emil transitions from hiding his crimes to orchestrating them for the camera, adopting a persona of calculated madness to manipulate legal outcomes and gain fame, with Oleg's filming becoming central to his strategy.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Oleg films Emil at the crime scene, marking the beginning of documented violence
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Emil is injured but insists on preserving the footage, now dependent on Cutler to manage his public narrative
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: the viability of Emil's Double Jeopardy plan is legally dubious and hinges on uncontrolled media exposure

cross_scene_arcs.title: Emil's Emotional Isolation and Search for Connection
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Despite escalating violence, Emil reveals moments of vulnerability—homesickness, partial remorse, and trauma—seeking connection through a Czech escort, threatening Daphne with conflicted emotion, and disclosing childhood abuse to gain empathy.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Emil expresses homesickness and requests a Czech woman from an escort service
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Emil reveals cigarette burn scars to Cutler, framing his past as torture to elicit validation
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: his emotional disclosures serve both genuine need and strategic manipulation, blurring authenticity

cross_scene_arcs.title: Securing Investigative Control
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Jordy asserts authority over the fire investigation amid jurisdictional tension with homicide, establishes forensic credibility, but is later excluded from key steps despite offering relevant cultural insight.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: challenging unauthorized homicide division access to the fire scene
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: dismissed by Eddie despite offering Polish heritage as investigative value
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: lack of clarity on who authorized early homicide involvement and ongoing marginalization in the investigation

cross_scene_arcs.title: Fixation and Pursuit of Daphne
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Jordy's professional interest in Daphne evolves into personal obsession, leading to emotional intimacy and protective behavior, culminating in shared danger during a fire.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: fixated on sketching Daphne, unable to sleep without it
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: trapped with Daphne in a burning apartment, injured while trying to protect her
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: relationship has no future due to Daphne's legal jeopardy and separation

cross_scene_arcs.title: Descent into Isolation and Crisis
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: After public vilification and personal loss, Jordy withdraws into isolation, experiences psychological collapse, and ultimately disobeys orders to kill Emil in a decisive act of personal justice.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: smashing phone in rage over media glorifying Emil Slovak
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: kills Emil after surrender, asserts identity as fire marshal not cop
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: legal and moral consequences of shooting Emil after he was no longer an immediate threat

cross_scene_arcs.title: Legacy and Rejection of Recognition
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Despite being publicly revealed as a hero and having saved lives, Jordy refuses media attention and symbolically passes Eddie's badge to Nicolette, choosing anonymity over acclaim.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: publicly identified as Fire Marshal Jordan Warsaw on live TV
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: declines interview, hands over Eddie's badge, and walks away silently
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: impact of his actions on Daphne and his own legal standing remain unclear
